reviews.title: THE FRONDESK STAFFS ARE DISSAPPOINTING
reviews.text: We booked this hotel through agoda, but I understand that the allotment is booking.com allotment, I know it cause I 'm working with online travel agent company. we arrived this hotel early morning around 4.30 after a long ride on the bus from Saigon, as soon as I arrived there, I thought I went to the wrong hotel as the lights at the lobby turned off, no one at the lobby or even security guard, I waited at the door and finally someone from the back of the hotel came and turned the lights on, clearly he had just woke up. we have requested for early check in at the time i made booking, he said the room is full now so I have to wait. I'm fine with it but then he checked on his computer, he said he could not find my booking and ask me to call the call center (that's the last thing you want to hear from the hotel staffs after a long ride on the bus), I was a bit frustrated asked him to check again, he confirmed that there's only one booking from booking.com today (that was mine actually, but no matter whose allotment it is but he should find my name there), after a long talk with call center and then he finally understood that it was my booking that I booked through agoda on booking,com allotment, no apologies from him. I asked what time can I check in , he said "I don't know", seriously? and then the other front desk staffs started working I explained my situation to them, again no apologies even from the manager.. they are not helpful and in need of education on how the system works and how to be helpful to the customer. there are lots of nice hotels around there that you can choose over this one.

reviews.title: Stay elsewhere!!!
reviews.text: I've stayed in hotels of all types all over Vietnam and this is by far the worst experience I have had in a hotel. If you want to be treated with dignity, stay somewhere else. The management is the worst I have ever seen and treat guests horribly, and they rip the guests of in their spa. What is more, the massage therapists are not therapists at all. My husband and I booked a 90 minute VIP hot stone massage with them. We only got a 60 minute massage (because of course the five minutes it takes to remove your clothing and wait for the masseuse counts as time toward the. Massage -- or so we were later told).  But thank god the massage was only 60 minutes. It was a revolting and humiliating experience. The therapists have absolutely no training. They seriously burned my husband, who still has burn marks on his back several days later. This lack of training was dangerous. Once the terrible experience was over (again, 30 mins early), they left us to dress. But the door has a big glass window without a covering. Several construction workers chose that particular moment to walk by our room, stop, look in, and see me completely naked. They then called others over to have a look. The spa workers did nothing to stop this. When we finished dressing we complained to both the spa staff and then then hotel staff and were met with indifference and disdain. We then sent several emails to the hotel management and here we're met with total silence. This is the worst I have ever been treated by a hotel, and a so called four star one at that. Do yourself a favor and don't submit to such humiliation.

reviews.title: Baddest hotel ever to me
reviews.text:
I stayed in from May 27th to May 30th, 2023. I'm going to talk about the terrible experience of May 28. I rented a motorbike that morning and it rained a lot on my way back to the hotel, so I got all wet.
I parked the motorbike in front of lobby at first, because of heavy rain poured down, and I contacted them to pick the motorbike up that I rented . But a hotel worker came out and told me need to park in the parking lot.
I've told you several times that it's not a motorbike that I'm going to park and it's a rented motorbike that the owner of motorbike shop pick it up in 10 minutes, but he ordered me to park on the other road. I asked if I could park on the road below it because I was frustrated because he didn't consider the customer's position at all in the rain, and he told me to do, so I parked on the road below, and he said to park further away. If it hadn't rained, I wouldn't have been angry, but I kept talking in a command tone when it was pouring down, so I didn't understand the customer's position in English and the road was not hotel land, so why did you order. When the front desk staff comes out, he listens to my explanation and tells me to understand because he can't speak English. Earlier, it was considered that he ordered well in English, and the front staff apologized when i said i would leave a review of the bad experience, saying that parking in a place other than the hotel's land could not be said.
And the hotel staff didn't even take issue with the Vietnamese parking at the hotel entrance. That makes me even angrier. And the hotel is very old, and there is a noise in the night market at night, and the noise in the hallway at night makes me unable to sleep. It feels like it's being well maintained, but it's never a new building.

reviews.title: Who the heck rated this hotel 4-stars? More like 2
reviews.text: If you were thinking to add breakfast to your booking, I suggest you to reconsider. The breakfast selections are below that of a 3-star hotel. It wasn't appetizing. The room size was too small, the room atmosphere was too dark. One spot light was installed in front of the bed, right above the small TV. That was the only lighting available for the room, except for what you get from the windows in the day. If you really have to, I recommend to stay here for a quick sleep only. There was a small, portable climate controller which it did not work when we turned on the A/C mode. It felt more like fan mode. We requested a nonsmoking room, booked 2 days ahead, and got a smoking room. The room smelled of cigarette smoke, the sheets were stained with the cigarette odor. I called the front desk, and they only offered to change the sheets. The entire room smelled of cigarette smoke, so we had to keep the windows opened the entire time, even at night. The room was not clean. The toilet seat had dried liquid spots (one could only guess it was urine spots). I did not enjoy my stay there. It was a quick stop, and I wouldn't pay this much money to stay there again. Certainly overpriced rooms. We were expecting more out of a 4-star hotel. It was more like a 2-stars hotel.

Lacking in simple needs like umbrellas, adaptors etc. Receptionist was lazy even at currency exchange, asked to change $110 but said only had enough VND to change for $100. reviews.title: Pretty on the outside but dated inside
reviews.text:
The TTC Hospitality hotel in Da Lat was by local standards quite expensive - *** for 2 ights.  Granted it was during Tet holiday, but I expected more from a 4 star rated hotel.  The location is great, the staff tries hard, but their efforts are not up to Western standards.  We checked in in the afternoon after a long day of travel and wanted to eat a bit so we went to their lobby restaurant.  Once again, it was about 3 pm, not the busiest time, but there were no wait staff and we had to seat ourselves and get soemone from guest relations to go to the back and tell the waitstaff that there were customers - still it took them  a long time to have a server ome to us.   They had many signs showing the prices of the fix priced meals available during the holiday.  For lunch, there were 4 options for the meals.  It was like buying a Ford Model T - you can have it in any color as long as it is black.  Lunch selection was any combonation available as long as it was combo number 4.  The food was pretty good though.  

The room was very dated, it definitely needs a remodel.  No air conditioning as many places in Da Lat are.  This made for a stuffy warm afternoon, but a more pleasant night as we opened up the balcony door and turned the fan on.  The only downfall was the local mosquito visitors that came in through the open door.

The bathroom had mildew around the shower a lot.

The morning buffet breakfast was very typical Vietnamese fare and so so quality.  The croissants tasted like they were left over from yesterday, but the fresh cooked egg station cooked my eggs to order perfectly.

Overall - Great location; Dated rooms, bathroom not so clean.

For me not worth the cost even for a holiday time.

reviews.title: Review of TTC Ngoc Lan Hotel, Da Lat
reviews.text:
Rating: ★★★☆☆

I stayed at TTC Nhoc Lan Hotel in Da Lat, and while there are some positives, my experience left me disappointed overall. The biggest downside was the room I was given, which faced an old building's garages. Not only was the view unpleasant, but the noise from the area, especially at night, made it hard to sleep. It felt like a constant disturbance from both people and vehicles nearby.

On the plus side, the hotel does offer a nice breakfast on the top floor with a beautiful view of the lake, which was a highlight of my stay. The gym on the ground floor was also decent, and the hotel's location is very convenient—close to bakeries, restaurants, stores, and public transport, making it easy to explore the area.

Overall, the hotel has its perks, but if you're looking for a peaceful stay with a better room view, I would recommend looking elsewhere. The noise and view were significant downsides, but the breakfast and location help give it a bit of a redeeming quality.

reviews.title: Good
reviews.text:
This was a fancy hotel but we were very disappointed. 
1) there was no air condition in the room .  Despite it being cooler in February, the room was kind of stuffy.  There is a sign saying not to open the windows because of mosquitoes.  So there was no way to get any kind of air flow.
2) Breakfast was mediocre. The breads were pasty.  Soup over salted.  
3) Pictures misleading.  It shows a nice spa, hot tub you have access to.  This is a paid service not included.  Its also in a very weird location.  You have to go to the basement and then thru a parking lot.  It is in a  basement parking garage.

The only thing this property had going for it was probably its location.  It is in the center of town close to the streets that have shopping.

I wouldn't stay here again, not for the price with no amenities.  This place is not a 4 star hotel.

reviews.title: An old hotel, facilities are used
reviews.text:
It was not a very pleasant stay.
First when we checked in at the count, the staff asked me if she could keep our passports. This was quite a shock for us, as the hotel should spontaneously make a photocopy rather than attempt to keep the passports until the guests check out.
Then when we enter the room, we found there's no AC, and the whether was hot enough for you to sweat in bed. There was only a small electric fan which had not much use for cooling down.
The hotel is just next to the night market, but this also goes with the problem of annoying noise at night. The soundproof was not so good and the roaring of motorbikes could be heard until midnight.
The breakfast was okay, but the fruits were just laid there on the plate, and we noticed dozens of flies consuming them before we do.

reviews.title: Uncomfortable and Scary!
reviews.text:
The check-in went smoothly, the moment you step out of the elevator to your floor, you'll soon get this errie feeling especially in the hotel room where everything feels aged and stories are embedded within the walls. The internet access is great with no limitations or proxy required. Don't even bother using the gym equipment as it's all outdated and utterly obsolete. The in-room dining and breakfast is surprisingly nice especially with the western-style food they had. Good value for money and recommend to think twice if you're sensitive to creaks and cracks!

Beware that there's no hotel room should you hire a chauffer to Da Lat so you'll might have to fork out some extra cash for his stay (tip: use a different hotel for the chauffer!)

reviews.title: Outstanding at night
reviews.text:
Pros
The hotel is right in the middle of Dalat city center and is the most outstanding hotel at night. Looks really grand on the outside. Location is great, you can access to night market, bike rental, lake and cafes pretty easily.  Rather peaceful at night. 

Cons 
Theres no aircon in the hotel, so you have to leave the balcony door open. If you are proned to mosquitoes bites, its better to keep an insect repellent with you, especially at night. The room is not sound proof and since you have to leave the door open for ventilation, it gets really noisy in the day time (night time is quite peaceful though). Hotel staffs not good in English, so communication is a problem most of the time. Nonetheless, they managed to help us book the airport shuttle bus, so its not that bad after all. Not much facilities. No view from the deluxe room, all you get is the backyard view of other hotels/ houses. 

Book this hotel if you don't mind the above mentioned. Anyways, Dalat was really FUN!

reviews.title: Anthon in TTC Hotel Premium Ngoc Lan, Dalat
reviews.text:
This Hotel and location would be perfect if some modifications were made. Rooms towards the City Market are noisy, and by no chance anyone with sleep troubles can fell asleep or get a decent rest, as the traffic outside and in close vicinity is horrific and continuously honking around the clock.

Double doors to the rooms to balcony do not help much as the outermost is like a blackener but without glass. The inner door has single glass, but the doors do not at all lock tight.
A modified door with double or triple glass would made a amazing difference and sure get guests to return to this otherwise very much OK nice Hotel.

Breakfast was very good and service as well very good.

Cleanliness and staffs profession is sure very good and whether I would return to the Hotel, the answer is yes, but with a room away from City indeed.
reviews.response:

Sincerely, 
TTC Hotel Premium Ngoc Lan

reviews.title: nice old hotel lots of character
Central.  Noisy.  Take your pick.
reviews.text:
The Ngoc Lan hotel is a large tourist class hotel right in the centre of Da Lat tourist area.  It is a minute's walk away from the night market and the lake.  The lake view is quite spectacular.  Check-in was flawless.  They DO charge extra for children.  They also keep your passports till checkout (standard practice in Da Lat apparently).  The room was very clean and spacious and furnishings new and comfortable.  The bathroom was clean and water pressure excellent.  Room service was fast and from the main restaurant and of good quality.  The breakfast buffet was very decent, with a 70/30 mix of local and international fare.  Wireless internet was unlocked and free and fairly fast.

The BAD part is that the hotel faces a LOUD NOISY BAR which plays awful music till about 11pm.  Closing both the windows and shutters helps but not enough.  Even after 11pm, being in the town centre and near the night market, you will sometimes hear vendors shouting as they pack and revelers chatting away.  The price you pay for being so central.  You can ask for a room facing the rear but the view is of a construction site.

I would come back again, but perhaps ask for the highest floor.

reviews.title: Great time with family but a few hiccups
reviews.text:
We booked two rooms in the hotel for our family. Our lake facing rooms were clean and decently equipped. There was local and international food just outside the hotel overlooking the Dalat Market. Also, transportation was a non-issue as taxi drivers were waiting outside the hotel for guests.

Annoyances: Although we were late, we had to wait for a while as one of our rooms was not ready even after we were brought to the room. Despite being told that there were construction works during the morning and in afternoon, a large crane lumbered up at 11pm and started lifting heavy stuff onto the roof which we could hear. We were later told by the staff that the law only permits such works to be carried out at night! Thankfully it was for one night only. The next day, hot and cold water supply was disrupted late at night and into the morning. My room was lucky was we got the supply back before breakfast. Other guests we spoke to were not so lucky and had to bathe in cold water. Perhaps this could be due to the works being carried out on the rooftop.

Tip: AVOID the hotel spa. Although cheap, the place is dark and has a strange smell. It gave us the creeps. It was an oversight on our part not to check out the facilities beforehand!
reviews.positives: Central Location, Clean Room, Access to Local Food

reviews.title: Best location in Dalat.
reviews.text:
we stayed for three nights in october 2012. the location has to be one of the best in dalat, right in the center of town, just opposite the central markets and overlooking the lake.
all the best restaurants and sights are within walking distance of the hotel. 
the hotel and staff are all very nice, it's quite new and modern.
we had a deluxe room which meant we got a very large room with a balcony, a big plus for us. the room has a nice sitting area that is separate from the sleeping area, for some reason the tv is in the sleeping area and not the sitting area which we found a little odd but other than that everything was really nice in the room.
the breakfast is pretty standard hotel fare, a bit dull but nothing wrong with it.
we asked for and got a non-smoking room, and that also is a big plus.

reviews.title: Great location and fantastic view.
reviews.text:
The hotel is situated in a very nice location. There are a lot interesting places that are within walking distance from the hotel.

In choosing a room, I recommended choosing the Lake View. It is especially well worth it since the sun rises on that side of the hotel. 

Their breakfast food selection was satisfactory but I would rather go outside and try the local food which tastes much better.

All throughout Dalat, there are only a few people who can communicate well in English. This hotel is no exception.
reviews.positives: Location, Lake View, Value for Money

reviews.title: Probably best located hotel in Dalat
reviews.text:
Large and modern hotel in prime location in Dalat. Very clean and with good facilities.  HUGE spa located in basement.
Some noise from the road outside (motorbike horns being blown constantly, like everywhere in Vietnam),
Breakfast was also not great - hot items were allowed to go cold.
Overall a good experience and would definitely use again if visiting Dalat.

reviews.title: Beautiful hotel in city centre
reviews.text:
This was a beautiful hotel. I had lake views, although the lake didn't have any water in it as it had mparky dried up. They are in the process of filling it up for Tet, which is the lunar new year celebration in the first week of February. 
Because of it's central location in the city of Dalat it is quite noisy all through the day and you can still hear cars at night. 
The "Doctor Fish" therapy is a must! It equates to just $2.50 US and is exclusive at this hotel. 
Overall, it was a very relaxing stay compared to the hustle and bustle of Ho Chi Minh city and Hanoi.
reviews.positives: Good location, newly renovated, spacious rooms

reviews.title: Nice hotel but expect better service
reviews.text:
I spent one night in Dalat at this hotel. The staff was accommodating while checking in, as I requested a quiet room as I suffer from chronic migraine. As they gave me the key they told me it was a quiet room. I got to the room door there was very loud construction going on above the room (hotel construction). I went back to reception and they changed my room to a “quieter” one. This time  I encountered construction noise coming from the balcony, as they are building a new building behind the TTC. Needless to say it wasn’t quiet but I didn’t want to go back downstairs w luggage again. 

The room was very clean, comfortable bed, no AC but it was chilly so didn’t need it. Breakfast was delicious, amazing croissants! 

Lastly when I asked the hotel how to get to my next destination they simply said, bus. I asked for help to find the schedule and book but they said “sorry I cannot help”. Even the concierge was no help. I would expect more from a big English speaking hotel like this.
